hero.stat:
In the last five years, the FDA rejected food shipments 47,250 times.
39% of all rejections were the result of paperwork errors or omissions.
hero.subhead: VeriPura checks your documents before the container moves. Not at the border.
tools.lead: Sign in with Google. Upload a document and see what the AI finds.
tools.core.name: VeriPura Core
tools.core.desc:
Upload a purchase order. Up to 50 orchestrated AI agents analyse the trade route, identify the regulatory framework, and determine every document required for that shipment.

Then upload your documents. Core validates each one, checks agreement across the full bundle, and tells you which documents are missing, out of date, or potentially fraudulent — and why.

tools.label.desc: Upload any food label. Instantly validate it against EU and US regulatory requirements.
tools.nutritional.name: VeriPura Nutritional
tools.nutritional.desc: Calculate and verify nutritional facts for recipes and formulations against market requirements.

faq.h1: Common questions about food import compliance verification.
faq.headerSub: Questions from food importers, freight forwarders, carriers, and customs brokers about how VeriPura works and where it fits.
faq.errors.eyebrow: Most common documentation errors caught by VeriPura
faq.errors.e1.title: Authority signature mismatch
faq.errors.e1.desc: Phytosanitary or health certificate signed by an authority not recognised by the destination market.
faq.errors.e2.title: FDA Prior Notice code error
faq.errors.e2.desc: Incorrect or missing product description code causes automatic detention by FDA PREDICT.
faq.errors.e3.title: TRACES NT format rejection
faq.errors.e3.desc: Common Entry Document format or missing field prevents TRACES NT acceptance before arrival.
faq.errors.e4.title: Expired certificate at arrival
faq.errors.e4.desc: Certificate valid at issue date but expired by the time the shipment reaches the border inspection post.
faq.errors.e5.title: Establishment number mismatch
faq.errors.e5.desc: Slaughter or processing establishment number does not match the destination market’s approved list.
faq.errors.e6.title: Missing EUDR due diligence records
faq.errors.e6.desc: No geolocation data or deforestation statement for EUDR-affected commodities — rejection at EU border.
faq.q1.a: VeriPura is a pre-shipment food import compliance verification service. It validates import documentation — phytosanitary certificates, health certificates, certificates of origin, certificates of analysis, and regulatory declarations — against EU, US, and UK border requirements before a container departs, using a 9-stage AI compliance workflow.
faq.q2.q: How does VeriPura prevent FDA automatic detentions?
faq.q2.a: FDA automatic detentions are typically triggered by documentation errors — an incorrect product code in Prior Notice, a missing field, or an authority mismatch. VeriPura checks every relevant field against FDA FSMA requirements before the shipment departs and flags issues for correction. A detention can cost $30,000–$50,000; the same error fixed before departure costs a corrected document.
faq.q3.q: Which import documents does VeriPura check?
faq.q3.a: VeriPura verifies phytosanitary certificates, health certificates, certificates of origin, certificates of analysis, common entry documents (CEDs), FDA prior notice submissions, fumigation certificates, and other documentation required by the destination market’s regulatory framework.
faq.q4.q: What regulatory frameworks does VeriPura cover?
faq.q4.a: VeriPura covers EU Regulation 2017/625, FDA FSMA, EUDR (EU Deforestation Regulation), FSMA 204 traceability requirements, TRACES NT, and UK FRCR. Additional frameworks are available on request.
faq.q5.q: How does VeriPura help freight forwarders?
faq.q5.a: Freight forwarders use VeriPura to batch-process compliance checks across multiple active shipments simultaneously. VeriPura flags expiry dates, missing signatures, and authority mismatches, and generates client-facing compliance reports automatically. Every check produces a blockchain-attested record — protecting forwarders from client disputes when shipments are rejected.
faq.q6.q: How do freight forwarders offer VeriPura compliance verification to their clients?
faq.q6.a: Freight forwarders join the VeriPura channel partner programme and offer pre-shipment document validation as a value-added service alongside their freight offering. When a client’s shipment is ready to move, the forwarder submits the document bundle through VeriPura. VeriPura checks every document against the destination market’s requirements — FDA FSMA, EUDR, TRACES NT, EU 2017/625, FSMA 204 — and returns a compliance verdict with any flagged issues before departure. The forwarder receives a blockchain-attested compliance record for every check performed, which can be provided to the importer client as part of the shipment dossier.
faq.q7.q: How does VeriPura help carriers and customs brokers reduce customs holds on food shipments?
faq.q7.a: Customs holds and port delays on food shipments are most commonly triggered by documentation errors that could have been caught before departure. VeriPura gives carriers and customs brokers a pre-arrival compliance check: document bundles are validated against the requirements of the destination port and regulatory regime before the vessel sails. Errors that would trigger an FDA detention, a TRACES NT rejection, or an EU border hold are identified and flagged while they are still fixable. VeriPura also produces a blockchain-attested audit trail for every check — giving customs brokers a documented record of compliance diligence if a shipment is later disputed.
faq.q8.q: What does the VeriPura channel partner programme offer freight forwarders and carriers?
faq.q8.a: The VeriPura channel partner programme gives freight forwarders and carriers three things: a pre-shipment compliance verification service they can offer food importer clients as a value-added product; a revenue share on every shipment verified through their channel; and a blockchain-attested compliance record for every check that materially reduces their liability exposure when clients face border disputes. Partner slots in the founding pilot are limited. Founding partners set the terms of the programme they want built — the corridors, document types, and integration with their existing systems that matter most.
faq.q9.q: How does VeriPura help with EUDR compliance for food imports?
faq.q9.a: The EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requires importers to demonstrate that specific commodities — including beef, soy, cocoa, coffee, and palm oil — have not contributed to deforestation. VeriPura checks EUDR due diligence documentation as part of its pre-shipment workflow, flagging gaps before departure.
faq.q10.q: How does the VeriPura workflow produce a compliance verdict?
faq.q10.a: VeriPura processes every shipment through 9 stages: Intake (document upload), Mapping (HS code and corridor identification), Parse (AI field extraction), Trace (supply chain linkage), Comply (EU and FDA rule check), Shield (risk scoring), Auditor (audit trail generation), Verdict (pass, fail, or fix with remediation guidance), and Ledger (IOTA blockchain anchor).
faq.q11.q: Is VeriPura a replacement for a customs broker or compliance consultant?
faq.q11.a: No. VeriPura automates document validation and compliance checking against known regulatory requirements. It is used by importers, forwarders, carriers, and brokers — not as a replacement for professional customs or legal advice. It handles the systematic, repeatable part of compliance: checking that the right documents are present, correctly completed, and consistent with each other and with regulatory requirements.
faq.q12.q: What is TRACES NT and why do food importers use it?
faq.q12.a: TRACES NT (Trade Control and Expert System) is the European Commission’s online platform for managing health and phytosanitary certificates for food imports entering the EU. Importers must submit Common Entry Documents (CEDs) via TRACES NT before shipments arrive at EU border inspection posts. Errors in document format, missing fields, or mismatches between the CED and the physical consignment are among the most common causes of EU food import refusals. VeriPura validates documentation against TRACES NT requirements before departure — so that CEDs are accepted on arrival rather than flagged for enhanced border checks.
faq.q13.q: What does FSMA 204 require from food importers, and how does VeriPura help?
faq.q13.a: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act Section 204 requires importers of designated high-risk foods to maintain and provide traceability records covering key data elements at each critical tracking event in the supply chain — from farm or processor through to the point of import. These records must be available to the FDA within 24 hours of a request. VeriPura validates that the required traceability documentation is present, correctly formatted, and consistent with the shipment before it departs — and anchors the records to the IOTA blockchain so they are immediately retrievable if FDA requests them.
faq.q14.q: Can VeriPura check the documents my supplier sends before I submit them to customs?
faq.q14.a: Yes. Supplier document validation is one of the primary use cases for VeriPura. Importers upload the document bundle received from their supplier — phytosanitary certificates, health certificates, certificates of origin, certificates of analysis — and VeriPura checks every field against the destination market’s requirements before the importer submits to customs or the shipment departs. Errors that would trigger a border rejection or FDA detention are caught at the point when they are still fixable: before the container moves.
faq.q15.q: What are the most common food import documentation errors that cause border rejections?
faq.q15.a: The most common documentation errors in food import compliance include: authority signature mismatches on phytosanitary or health certificates; incorrect or missing product description codes in FDA Prior Notice submissions; Common Entry Document format errors that TRACES NT rejects; cold chain log formats not accepted by destination market authorities; establishment numbers that do not match approved lists; certificates that were valid at issue but expired by the time the shipment arrives; and missing EUDR due diligence records for affected commodities. VeriPura checks for all of these against the specific requirements of the destination market and trade corridor before the shipment departs.
faq.q16.q: Which food commodities are affected by the EU Deforestation Regulation and what documentation does it require?
faq.q16.a: The EUDR applies to cattle, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soy, wood, and derived products — including beef, chocolate products, coffee blends, palm oil derivatives, and soy-based ingredients commonly traded in food import shipments. Importers must provide due diligence statements demonstrating that the commodities were produced on land not subject to deforestation after December 2020, with supporting geolocation data and supply chain documentation. VeriPura checks that the required EUDR due diligence documentation is present and complete before departure, flagging gaps that would cause rejection at EU borders.
faq.cta.text: Still have questions? Talk to Onno and Thomas directly.
usecases.headerSub: Illustrative scenarios across the food categories most exposed to documentation failure at European, US, and UK borders.
usecases.headerNote: All shipment scenarios are illustrative examples, not real shipments or clients.
usecases.rejectedDoc.h2: This is what a border rejection looks like.
usecases.rejectedDoc.p:
A single signature mismatch on a phytosanitary certificate. An incorrect product code in FDA Prior Notice. A cold chain log format TRACES NT won’t accept. Each one costs tens of thousands of dollars and days at port.

Every scenario below was preventable. The error existed in the documents before the container moved. VeriPura finds it at Intake — not at Rotterdam, Hamburg, or Tilbury.
usecases.callout.title: Authority Signature Mismatch
usecases.callout.body: Certificate signatory not on Rotterdam accepted authority list.
usecases.h2: Four corridors. Four failures that didn’t have to happen.
usecases.uc1.title: Thai Mango to Rotterdam
usecases.uc1.scenario: A Dutch importer sources 12 containers of fresh mango from a Thai cooperative. The shipment requires a phytosanitary certificate, pesticide residue analysis, a certificate of origin, and pre-export TRACES notification — all issued by different Thai authorities, in Thai and English.
usecases.uc1.pain: Without VeriPura: Authority signature mismatch on phytosanitary cert caught at Rotterdam border control. 6-day hold. Substantial demurrage, re-inspection fees, and product loss — mango downgraded and partially destroyed.
usecases.uc1.outcome: With VeriPura: Signature mismatch flagged at Intake stage. Corrected cert obtained before departure. Container clears Rotterdam on arrival. Zero demurrage.
usecases.uc1.hook: → This is the kind of shipment VeriPura was built for.
usecases.uc1.hookLink: Apply for a pilot slot — we verify EU phytosanitary documentation
usecases.uc2.title: Vietnamese Shrimp to Hamburg
usecases.uc2.scenario: A German seafood distributor imports 8 tonnes of frozen shrimp from a Vietnamese processor. Documentation required includes health certificates, catch certificates, cold chain logs, HACCP compliance records, and EU import notification via TRACES NT — across 5 issuing authorities.
usecases.uc2.pain: Without VeriPura: Cold chain log format not accepted by TRACES NT system. Shipment flagged for enhanced border checks. 4-day delay, substantial costs incurred. Buyer relationship damaged.
usecases.uc2.outcome: With VeriPura: Cold chain log format issue identified at Parse stage. Supplier provided corrected format within 24 hours. Shipment pre-cleared via TRACES before vessel arrival.
usecases.uc2.hook: → This is the kind of shipment VeriPura was built for.
usecases.uc2.hookLink: Apply for a pilot slot — we verify TRACES NT compliance before departure
usecases.uc3.title: Indian Spices to US FDA
usecases.uc3.scenario: A US specialty food importer sources cumin, turmeric, and chilli powder from three Indian suppliers. Each shipment requires FDA Prior Notice, FSMA traceability records, a Certificate of Analysis, and fumigation certificates — with strict field formatting required for FDA PREDICT system acceptance.
usecases.uc3.pain: Without VeriPura: Prior Notice entry rejected by FDA PREDICT due to incorrect product description code. Shipment placed on automatic detention. Storage, re-examination, and bond costs in the range of $30K–$50K.
usecases.uc3.outcome: With VeriPura: Product code mismatch identified at Comply stage before submission. Corrected Prior Notice accepted first time. No detention. FSMA traceability records blockchain-attested for audit.
usecases.uc3.hook: → This is the kind of shipment VeriPura was built for.
usecases.uc3.hookLink: Apply for a pilot slot — we verify FDA Prior Notice and FSMA 204 records
usecases.uc4.title: Brazilian Beef to Great Britain
usecases.uc4.scenario: A British meat importer sources chilled and frozen beef cuts from a Brazilian processor in Mato Grosso do Sul. The shipment requires a UK health certificate issued by MAPA, an Export Health Certificate validated by APHA, deforestation due diligence records under the UK Forest Risk Commodities framework, cold chain documentation, and an EU–Mercosur-aligned certificate of origin.
usecases.uc4.pain: Without VeriPura: APHA rejects the Export Health Certificate at Tilbury — slaughter establishment number on the health cert does not match the approved UK establishment list. Shipment held for 9 days. Significant costs in cold storage, re-inspection, and partial product loss.
usecases.uc4.outcome: With VeriPura: Establishment number mismatch and deforestation documentation gap both flagged at Comply stage before departure from Santos. Corrected certificates obtained within 48 hours. Shipment arrives Tilbury pre-cleared. IOTA-attested deforestation records available to UK Border Force on request — zero manual chase.
usecases.uc4.hook: → This is the kind of shipment VeriPura was built for.
usecases.uc4.hookLink: Apply for a pilot slot — we verify UK import health certificates and EUDR records
usecases.bottomCta.h2: Ready to stop this happening to your shipments?
usecases.bottomCta.p: Five importer slots. Two partner slots. We run a live pilot against your real shipments — at no cost to you.
partners.h1: The food import compliance partner programme built for freight forwarders and carriers.
partners.headerSub: Every compliance failure your importer clients suffer is a cost you absorb — in delays, disputes, and damaged relationships. VeriPura turns that liability into a competitive advantage and a new revenue stream.
partners.slots.label: Partner slots remaining
partners.prog.h2: Three things the programme gives you.
partners.prog.lead: Freight forwarders and carriers that add VeriPura compliance verification to their service offering give food importer clients a pre-shipment document validation check against FDA FSMA, EUDR, TRACES NT, and EU 2017/625 requirements — reducing customs holds and border rejections before the container moves.
partners.card1.title: Revenue Share on Every Shipment
partners.card1.desc: Channel partners earn a share of VeriPura subscription and per-shipment attestation revenue for every client they introduce. Each new importer client you onboard generates recurring income — not a one-time referral fee.
partners.card2.title: Protect Yourself from Client Liability
partners.card2.desc: When a shipment is rejected at the border, the importer looks to their forwarder first. VeriPura gives you a documented, blockchain-attested record of every compliance check performed — materially reducing your exposure when clients face border disputes.
partners.card3.title: Extend Your Service Offering
partners.card3.desc: VeriPura compliance validation becomes a value-added service you offer alongside freight. Clients sourcing from high-risk corridors — Thailand, Vietnam, Brazil, India — need this capability now, with EUDR and FSMA 204 enforcement live. You become their single point of contact for compliant cross-border food trade.

partners.pilot.p: Founding partners help shape the channel programme, complete the pilot at no cost, and receive preferential pricing when they convert — terms not available to later partners. What that looks like is a conversation.
partners.howit.h2:
From client shipment to compliance record
in five steps.
partners.howit.lead: When your importer client has a shipment ready to move, you submit their document bundle through VeriPura. The check runs against the destination market’s specific requirements. You receive a verdict — and a blockchain-attested record — before the container departs.
partners.step1.title: Client submits document bundle
partners.step1.desc: Phytosanitary certificates, health certificates, certificates of origin, certificates of analysis, FDA Prior Notice, and any other documents required for the destination market.
partners.step2.title: VeriPura maps corridor and frameworks
partners.step2.desc: HS code identification, origin/destination corridor, and applicable regulatory regimes — EU 2017/625, FDA FSMA, EUDR, TRACES NT, FSMA 204, UK FRCR — are identified automatically.
partners.step3.title: AI compliance check runs across all documents
partners.step3.desc: 50+ specialised agents check every field — authority signatures, product codes, establishment numbers, certificate validity dates, EUDR due diligence records — against the requirements of the destination market.
partners.step4.title: Verdict delivered with remediation guidance
partners.step4.desc: Pass, fail, or fix. Any flagged issues include specific remediation instructions — so errors are corrected at the point when they are still fixable: before the container moves.
partners.step5.title: Blockchain-attested compliance record issued
partners.step5.desc: Every check is anchored to the IOTA blockchain, producing an immutable, timestamped audit trail. You provide this to your importer client as part of the shipment dossier — and retain it as documented evidence of compliance diligence.
partners.corridors.h2: High-risk corridors where documentation failures concentrate.
partners.corridors.lead: VeriPura is built for the origin–destination pairs where documentation errors cause the most border rejections and FDA detentions.
partners.bottomCta.h2:
Two founding partner slots.
Terms set by those who fill them.
partners.bottomCta.p: Founding partners complete the pilot at no cost and receive preferential pricing that is not available to later joiners. They also shape the corridors, document types, and integrations that get built first. What that looks like in practice is a 30-minute conversation.
partners.bottomCta.btn2: Book a Call with Onno or Thomas
